vision.notes: Both views render a recognizable flèche: a tall, slender octagonal (faceted) shaft tapering monotonically to a fine needle, with clear staging — gilt souche band at the base, the long fût, intermediate gallery rings, and the needle. Oxidized-copper (green) statuary projects from a gallery, and a small gilt element (coq/orb) sits at the ~96m summit. The extreme slenderness is consistent with the measured 96m proportions and is NOT penalized per V08/V09. Minor deductions only: the statuary reads as a single clustered, low-poly group biased to the front rather than four distinct apostle/evangelist groups distributed around the gallery corners, and the cluster sits a bit high on the fût rather than hugging the base gallery. Core morphology, staging, statuary, and summit coq are all present and faithful.
